title: Whakairo Whenua: Designing Sustainable Land Management Systems | Te Whakamahi i te Mātauranga Taketake ki te Whakahaere Whenua
subject: Technology
culturalContext: Students will explore traditional Ngāti Kahungunu land management practices used along the Mangakōtukutuku Stream, focusing on how their tūpuna used technology to create pā tuna (eel weirs), maintain water quality, and sustainably harvest resources. The context connects to the college's namesake stream and the ongoing restoration work by local hapū.
learningObjectives: Design and create a scale model of a traditional pā tuna system using natural materials and explain its technological principles
Analyze water quality data from Mangakōtukutuku Stream using digital tools and compare with traditional monitoring methods
Collaborate with local kaumātua to document and present one traditional land management technology used by Ngāti Kahungunu
Evaluate how modern technology can enhance traditional practices for environmental sustainability

activities.title: Whakapapa o te Awa
activities.description: Visit Mangakōtukutuku Stream with kaumātua to hear pūrākau about the awa's significance and observe traditional land management features. Students use digital cameras and mapping apps to document observations.

activities.title: Hanga Pā Tuna
activities.description: In groups, students design and construct scale models of traditional eel weirs using natural materials (harakeke, toetoe, wood). They test water flow and functionality, applying technological principles of structure and forces.

activities.title: Mātai Wai Māori
activities.description: Using water testing kits and digital sensors, students collect and analyze water quality data from the stream. They compare results with traditional indicators (presence of tuna, clarity, plant health) documented through interviews.

activities.title: Hui ā-Iwi
activities.description: Students prepare and present their findings to local kaumātua and community members, demonstrating how traditional knowledge and modern technology can work together for sustainable land management.

resources: Local kaumātua from Ngāti Kahungunu (e.g., Uncle Wiremu Williams)
Mangakōtukutuku Stream water testing kits and digital pH/temperature sensors
'Ngā Whakaraupapa: Traditional Māori Technologies' by Dr. Pauline Harris
Te Papa Tongarewa online collection of traditional fishing implements

tasks: Design portfolio showing pā tuna model development with technical drawings
Water quality analysis report comparing traditional and modern monitoring methods
Oral presentation to community stakeholders with digital slides
Reflective journal on cultural and technological learning throughout the unit

nzcAlignment: Technological Practice: Planning for practice, brief development, and outcome evaluation
Nature of Technology: Characteristics of technology and how it evolves
Technological Knowledge: Technological modelling and products
Key Competencies: Participating and contributing, relating to others
